title: Earthdata Search
description: Discover how NASA's Earth Science data search platform is built — from React UI to serverless backend.

steps.title: NASA's Data Discovery Platform
steps.description: Earthdata Search lets scientists find, visualize, and download Earth Science data from NASA's Common Metadata Repository. The frontend lives in static/, the serverless backend in serverless/, and shared logic bridges both.

steps.title: React Frontend
steps.description: 98 components power the UI — search results, an interactive map, granule browsing, and data access workflows. Zustand manages state through 21 domain slices, and Apollo Client handles GraphQL queries to the backend.

steps.title: Zustand State Architecture
steps.description: Instead of Redux, state is split into 21 focused slices — collections, granules, map, project, query, user, and more. Each slice owns its domain logic with immer for immutable updates, composed into a single store.

steps.title: Serverless Lambda Backend
steps.description: 30+ Lambda functions handle data access orchestration — ordering through CMR, Harmony, and SWODLR services, plus auth via Earthdata Login, shapefile storage, and a GraphQL API that the frontend queries.

steps.title: Multi-Tenant Portal System
steps.description: One codebase powers 25+ portals — PODAAC, GHRc, ORNL DAAC, and more. Each portal config customizes branding, default search params, and feature toggles, letting data centers offer tailored search experiences from shared code.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: React frontend, serverless backend, shared utilities, CDK infrastructure, and a portal system — all composing NASA's primary Earth Science data discovery tool serving thousands of researchers worldwide.
